subject: What you need for iPaq wireless connectivity (Tech Update)
body:
Tech Update Today

VITAL SIGNS FOR JULY 12, 2002

David Berlind
What you need for iPaq wireless connectivity
A public health organization has several iPaqs on order, butnow they want to add WAN connectivity to those PDAs. Whatexactly do they need to do? I'm so glad they asked.
Here's everything you need to know

Latest from ZDNet News

Security holds back Web services

China: Bursting with brainpower

Micron cranks handle on next-gen memory

Japan chipmakers team on 'SoCs' chip

HP tech exec jumps ship to EMC

Holes put Linux handheld at risk

Microsoft bids to be creme de la CRM

More Enterprise News

Farber's Picks
Dan
Farber Desperately seeking chief ethics officer
Greedy CEOs are making a bad name for the profession--and corporate America. The title chief executive officer nowconjures up an image of someone with questionable ethics, agreedy disposition, and a disregard for shareholders. Ifgovernment-proposed remedies can"t adequately address thisethics crisis, disgruntled shareholders surely will.
Read my full commentary Oracle challenges Microsoft anew
Oracle is taking on Microsoft's Exchange with a Collaboration Suite that includes e-mail, calendar and messaging software. Oracle has pitched its products as Microsoft replacements before -- with spectacularly little success. (At Comdex last November, the database giant offered its 9i and app-server software as a more secure alternative to Exchange.) What's different this time? Oracle is selling a separate collaboration product that, says CEO Larry Ellison, is cheaper than rival offerings. Plus: Companies facing a deadline to join Redmond's new licensing program may be more open to non-Micrososft alternatives.
Read the full story
Also: Ellison shrugs a No. 2 exec Standards stalled over royalty disputes
The World Wide Web Consortium is bracing for a vote next week that could decide once and for all how the standards body will handle patented technology that comes with royalties attached. If patented technology is rejected, the vote could mark a major shift in the Internet standards battle. Major intellectual property holders such as Microsoft and IBM have a lot at stake.
Read the full story
Microsoft's OpenGL claim shakes graphics world Survey: Execs rethinking IT budgets
Growing numbers of Fortune 1000 CIOs are re-evaluating their IT budgets and spending plans for coming months, according to a recent survey. Morgan Stanley finds that CIOs' top priorities include security software, application integration and Windows 2000/XP upgrades for the desktop. Integration challenges were slowing the rate of application adoption for 38 percent of those surveyed.
Read more survey resultsOh look, a new bug in Outlook and IE
A Danish security expert has warned of a security flaw in Microsoft's ActiveX scripting component that allows network attackers to peek at cookies, read files and even run programs on a victim's PC. The expert recommended that users disable ActiveX in IE and Outlook, at least until Microsoft releases a patch.
Read the full story
Also: Outlook plug-in opens PCs to hackersWrite me at dan.farber@cnet.com

Back to top

COMMENTARY
Mixing Unix and Windows
Most enterprises have a mix of Windows and Unix systems. Larry Seltzer explores the options for interoperating those systems.

Q&A
Marcelo Tosatti: The future is Linux
The maintainer of the Linux kernel talks about new opportunities, defends UnitedLinux and tells why copyright laws keep him from entering the U.S.

PREVIOUSLY ON TECH UPDATE TODAY
ROI calculators don't tell the whole TCO story
In response to David Berlind's "Dell's formula for ROI," TCOconsultant Tom Rankin warns that "99 percent of TCO tools donot look at the 'total' but may look at whatever parameterssuit the vendor to sell their products/services."

subject: SALT | News from the pier
body:
NEWS FROM THE PIER!

We are approaching the end of winter and a spring with a packed program! Check out these news and highlights for the upcoming period.

OSLO HUMORFEST | FEBRUARY 26 – 28
Next Thursday we kick off with perhaps Norway's most fun festival, OSLO HUMORFEST 🔥
Welcome to three days of fooling around, juggling, clowning, standup, and everything else that is absolutely hilariously funny! To join Oslo Humorfest you need either a day pass or a single ticket to the show you want to see.

MIND ENTERPRISES (IT) | MARCH 11
The up-and-coming Italian duo Mind Enterprises comes to Norway for the first time! On March 11, they bring their Italian disco to SALT 🇮🇹🪩

CONSTRUCTION WORK ON THE PREMISES
In December, SALT received approval from the Planning and Building Agency to remain on the site until the end of 2029 – with good opportunities for extension. Now we are initiating several major rebuilding projects to further develop the area. Unfortunately, this will entail some construction work for a period, and Naustet sauna must therefore remain temporarily closed.

BINGOBAR IS BACK – EASTER EDITION & 80'S EDITION
BingoBar is back with two fantastic special editions you won't want to miss! First up is Easter Edition, then we turn back time with the 80's Edition! Neon, shoulder pads, power ballads and MORE IS MORE.

FOUR INTERNATIONAL CONCERTS WE ARE LOOKING FORWARD TO!
IDA-LOVA (SE) | 20.02 (TONIGHT!)
VÆB (IS) | 21.02
STUZZI (SE) | 06.03
THE ROOP (LT) | 20.03

CULTURAL PROGRAM FOR THE COMING WEEKS

subject: Cyber Week: You Could Win! See Inside to Get your Temu Rewards

body:
One of the top nutrition researchers in the world has just reported a surprising discovery. And it may prove life-changing for middle-aged and older Americans.

It’s becoming common for people over 40 to say they suddenly feel like they have the body of someone 20 years older. Telltale signs include gas, bloating, joint stiffness, daytime fatigue, and unwanted weight gain.

“Contrary to popular belief, these are not natural problems. Not at age 40, 50, or even 80. We think we’ve discovered why it’s happening,” says Dr. Steven Gundry — one of Castle Connolly’s Top American Doctors.

According to Dr. Gundry’s research, there’s one "anti-nutrient" that’s become widespread in American food. And this unhealthy compound — rather than just "age" — is often behind the digestive, energy, and weight problems people are having.

"Unfortunately, this compound is in nearly everything we eat at this point," Dr. Gundry continues. "Since it’s nearly impossible to avoid, we’ve had to come up with a different solution."

Fortunately, after working with thousands of patients, Dr. Gundry discovered there was one food that could actually block your body from absorbing the compound in the first place.

He found, if you added this one food to your diet... you could start getting back your energy, relieving digestive issues... and even losing weight more easily.

In fact, when Dr. Gundry had a pilot group of people try this, nearly all of them reported feeling a big difference by the first check-in a few weeks later.

They described benefits like a flatter belly, looser clothes, more mental alertness, smoother digestion, increased confidence, and feeling “lighter” overall. *

Since then, Dr. Gundry has recommended this surprisingly simple solution to hundreds of patients at his clinic. And after seeing the dramatic difference it's made in their weight, energy levels, and overall health... he now strongly encourages all adults over the age of 40 to follow suit.

So, with the help of a video team, he’s put together a short online report. In it, he reveals the toxic compound in food that causes problems in your body. And he shows you the food you can eat to block it.

Watch The Video


Dr. Gundry does caution that everyone’s body is different. And the results you experience may vary. However, the feedback on the technique has been overwhelmingly positive. "The best part is that it only takes about a minute a day to do. I encourage everyone to try it,” says Dr. Gundry.

subject: Addressing an Emergent Quantum Threat
body:
Addressing an Emergent Quantum Threat

Dear Ledger User,

We are contacting you today regarding a critical development in digital asset security that requires your immediate attention. Our research and threat intelligence teams have identified a paradigm shift in potential attack vectors, moving from theoretical concepts to practical threats.

Specifically, we have found credible evidence of malicious actors leveraging emergent quantum computational power. These attacks are designed to exploit the underlying cryptographic principles—namely the Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm (ECDSA)—that secure most digital wallets. In essence, this advanced capability could allow sophisticated actors to derive a private key from a public address, an action previously considered computationally impossible.

We believe this new threat vector is responsible for a series of recent, unexplained fund thefts from various wallets across the ecosystem. To be clear: this is not a vulnerability in Ledger's hardware design, but an industry-wide challenge to the very foundation of current cryptographic standards.

Post-Quantum Firmware Update

To safeguard your holdings against this threat, we have released a mandatory firmware update. This is not a routine patch; it is a proactive cryptographic upgrade that integrates post-quantum cryptographic (PQC) countermeasures into your device's core architecture. This will fortify your hardware wallet against both current and future quantum attacks.

It is imperative that you install this update immediately to ensure the continued integrity of your assets. To update:

Ledger has always been at the forefront of digital asset security. Our mission requires us to anticipate and neutralize threats before they achieve widespread impact. This firmware update is a testament to that commitment.

Update Firmware via Ledger Live

Thank you for your immediate cooperation.

Sincerely,
The Ledger Cryptography & Security Team

Ledger SAS, 1 Rue du Mail, 75002 Paris, France

This is a critical security notification. Always verify the authenticity of such requests by launching Ledger Live directly and never from a link in an email you do not trust.

JETZT SPIELEN


Das Angebot gilt nur für neue Spieler. Glücksspiel ist eine Freizeitbeschäftigung. Unabhängig davon, ob Sie oder jemand, den Sie kennen, sich für das Spielen entscheidet, ist es wichtig zu verstehen, dass das Glücksspiel mit potenziellen Risiken verbunden ist. Achten Sie auf Anzeichen wie ein dringendes Bedürfnis weiterspielen zu wollen, die Häufigkeit des Spielens zu erhöhen, Geld für die Aktivität zu leihen oder sich deswegen unglücklich zu fühlen. Intelligentes und sicheres Spielen ist sehr wichtig. Wenn Sie erste Anzeichen von Sucht erkennen, zögern Sie nicht, um Hilfe zu bitten.
